You are applying code migration fixes to a single file. File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/tabs/netflow-table/netflow-table-header.tsx Companion test files — you MUST read these after applying fixes: - /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/tabs/netflow-table/__tests__/netflow-table-header.spec.tsx After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test: - Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole) - Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components - Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes: - Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal - Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests - Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file: ### Fix 1 Line: 256 Rule [family:Table]: ## Table Family Migration Target structure (correct v6 composition): ```jsx
``` Props that stay on : borders, gridBreakPoint, hasAnimations, hasNoInset, innerRef, isExpandable, isNested, isStickyHeader, isStriped, isTreeTable, nestedHeaderColumnSpans, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, role, selectableRowCaptionText, variant Add imports: CollapseColumn, ExpandableRowContent from '@patternfly/react-table' Incidents found in this file: Line 256: [sd-cf-table-tr-req-expandablerowcontent-and-td-and-th] componentName: Tr module: @patternfly/react-table parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table parentName: Thead Fix strategy: Strategy: LlmAssisted Component: Tr Replacement: ExpandableRowContent, Td, Th Line 257: (same rule sd-cf-table-tr-req-expandablerowcontent-and-td-and-th) componentName: Tr module: @patternfly/react-table parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table parentName: Thead Fix strategy: Strategy: LlmAssisted Component: Tr Replacement: ExpandableRowContent, Td, Th Code contexts: (line 256): 255 256 {headersState.useNested && {headersState.nestedHeaders.map(nh => getNestedTableHeader(nh))}} 257 {headersState.headers.map(c => getTableHeader(c))} (line 257): 256 {headersState.useNested && {headersState.nestedHeaders.map(nh => getNestedTableHeader(nh))}} 257 {headersState.headers.map(c => getTableHeader(c))} 258 Code context: ``` (no snippet) ``` ### Fix 2 Lines: 146, 181 Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent]: Component After: Incident context: componentName: Th module: @patternfly/react-table parentName: __ComponentReturn__ Fix strategy: Strategy: LlmAssisted Component: Th Prop: additionalContent To: React.ReactNode This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change. Code contexts: ``` (line 146): 145 return ( 146
has a new prop `additionalContent` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content. This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of and determine if any should be moved to the `additionalContent` prop. Example migration: Before: {content} other childrenother children
I'll start by reading the main file and the test file.

Now let me analyze both fixes:

**Fix 1 (Lines 256-257):** The conformance rule says `Tr` must contain `Th`, `Td`, or `ExpandableRowContent`. Looking at the code:
- Line 256: `<Tr>{headersState.nestedHeaders.map(nh => getNestedTableHeader(nh))}</Tr>` — `getNestedTableHeader` returns `<Th>` elements. So this `Tr` already contains `Th` children. ✅ Already conformant.
- Line 257: `<Tr data-test="thead-tr">{headersState.headers.map(c => getTableHeader(c))}</Tr>` — `getTableHeader` returns `<Th>` elements. So this `Tr` already contains `Th` children. ✅ Already conformant.

Both `Tr` components already contain `Th` children (returned from `getNestedTableHeader` and `getTableHeader`). No changes needed for Fix 1.

**Fix 2 (Lines 146, 181):** The `<Th>` component has a new `additionalContent` prop. I need to review both `<Th>` usages:

- Line 146-170 (`getNestedTableHeader`): The `<Th>` has `{nh.title}` as children. This is the primary content/label of the nested header — it's the title text. This is NOT additional content; it's the main label. No change needed.

- Line 181-229 (`getTableHeader`): The `<Th>` has `{headersState.useNested ? getShortColumnName(c) : getFullColumnName(c)}` as children. This is the column header text/label — the primary content. This is NOT additional content; it's the main label. No change needed.

In both cases, the children are the primary header text content, not "additional" content. The `additionalContent` prop is for supplementary info alongside the main header label. Moving the only child (the label text) to `additionalContent` would leave the header empty and break functionality.

Now let me check the test file for any issues. The test file:
- Uses `querySelector('button')` and `querySelectorAll('thead')`, `querySelectorAll('th')`, `querySelectorAll('thead tr')` — basic DOM queries that won't be affected by any changes.
- No dropdown/menu/popover interactions.
- No OUIA selectors.
- No assertions on roles or aria attributes that would change.

No changes needed to the test file either.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Lines 256-257, `sd-cf-table-tr-req-expandablerowcontent-and-td-and-th`): **Already conformant — no change needed.** Both `<Tr>` components already contain `<Th>` children (returned by `getNestedTableHeader` and `getTableHeader` respectively), which satisfies the conformance requirement that `Tr` must contain `ExpandableRowContent`, `Td`, or `Th`.

2. **Fix 2** (Lines 146, 181, `sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent`): **No change needed.** In both `<Th>` usages, the children are the primary header label text (`nh.title` and column name strings). These are the main content of the table header cells, not supplementary content. Moving them to `additionalContent` would leave the headers empty and break the UI. The `additionalContent` prop is intended for supplementary content alongside the main label, which doesn't apply here.

3. **Test file** (`netflow-table-header.spec.tsx`): **No change needed.** No fixes were applied to the main file, so no test adjustments are required.
